macro_segments.title: The Case of Giddul bar I'lai and the Dispute Between Abaye and Rava on Gilluy Da'as

micro_segments.title: Narrative: Giddul bar I'lai sends a get and then regrets it
Abaye's position: gilluy da'as is not sufficient to nullify the get or agency
Rava's position: gilluy da'as is sufficient to nullify the agency
The dispute: whether revealing intent affects the shlichus or the get itself

macro_segments.title: Rava's Proof from Rav Sheshet's Forced Divorce Case

micro_segments.title: Rav Sheshet forces a man to divorce; the man claims Rav Sheshet said to nullify the get
Rava argues that the man's false statement about Rav Sheshet shows gilluy da'as was enough

macro_segments.title: Abaye's Rebuttal: The Man Actually Performed Bitul

micro_segments.title: Abaye reinterprets: the man actually did perform bitul, not merely reveal intent
The lashes forced him to make the false claim about Rav Sheshet

macro_segments.title: Abaye's Proof from Rav Yehuda's Plugged-Ears Case

micro_segments.title: Rav Yehuda forces a man repeatedly to say yes and write a get, plugging witnesses' ears
Abaye's inference: you need formal retraction that can be prevented by blocking ears

macro_segments.title: Rava's Response and Additional Cases on Conditional Gets

micro_segments.title: Rava reinterprets the plugged-ears case: the man may be saying 'give it to her quickly'
Case of get given on condition of return within thirty days; man calls out but can't cross
Shmuel rules the condition was satisfied despite the man's clear intent not to divorce
Case of get on condition of nissuin within thirty days; man falsely claims different month
Debate applied: whether gilluy da'as suffices when get is conditional
Case of get on condition of nissuin by Rosh Chodesh Adar; man misremembers as Nissan

macro_segments.title: The Halacha: Ruling Like Abaye That Gilluy Da'as Does Not Suffice

micro_segments.title: The halacha rules like Abaye on multiple disputes, including gilluy da'as
Explanation of why Abaye was called Nachmani

macro_segments.title: Mishna: Multiple Names and Tikkun Olam in Get Documents

micro_segments.title: Original practice: changing names or cities; Rabban Gamliel's reform to include all names

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Case of People Known by Different Names in Different Regions

micro_segments.title: Question: person known as Yosef in one place, Yochanan in another—how to write get?
Rav Ashi: only required if two names are established; not required if only one is known

macro_segments.title: Braita: Man with Two Wives in Two Regions and Two Names—Validity Rules

micro_segments.title: Case: man has two wives (one in Judea, one in Galilee) and two names; divorce using only local name
Ruling: get is not valid unless both names are included
Question: why different if man leaves to a third place?
Answer: in third place the other names are not established, so get is valid with one name

macro_segments.title: Case of Woman Known by Majority Name: Default Name Should Be Used

micro_segments.title: Case: woman known as both Miriam and Sarah; use the majority name (Miriam)

topical_tags.term: known by different names in different places
